articles.title: A tech worker in China is laid off and replaced by AI. Is it legal?
articles.summary: A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.

articles.title: The U.S. once had a monopoly on rare earths. How it lost to China
articles.summary: Planet Money tells the story of how the U.S. lost to China on rare earths.

articles.title: Federal Appeals Court Temporarily Halts Abortion Pill Access by Mail
articles.summary: The court order, in a lawsuit by the state of Louisiana, pauses a Food and Drug Administration regulation that greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

articles.title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
articles.summary: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

articles.title: Eric Swalwell Was Proficient in Social Media. So Were His Accusers.
articles.summary: Eric Swalwell used social media to boost his image and meet women, accusers said. Some of those same women used apps and influencers to join forces years later.

articles.title: Court restricts abortion access across the US by blocking the mailing of mifepristone
articles.summary: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.

articles.title: Trump Tells Congress Why He Doesn’t Need Its Authorization for the Iran War
articles.summary: In letters to the House and Senate, the president asserted that the hostilities had “terminated,” in an apparent attempt to avoid having to seek congressional approval.

articles.title: Trump Says He’s ‘Not Satisfied’ With Iran’s Latest Proposal
articles.summary: Also, Spirit Airlines prepares to shut down. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

articles.title: F.D.A. Grants Early Access to Promising Drug for Pancreatic Cancer
articles.summary: Patients with one of the deadliest cancers have been pleading for an unapproved treatment that may prolong their lives.

articles.title: AI robot tech bros dogs run wild in Berlin gallery
articles.summary: In Germany, robotic AI dogs with the faces of tech's most powerful men are on the loose — courtesy of American artist Beeple.

articles.title: Iran war shakes Global economy as energy costs surge and recession fears grow
articles.summary: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.

articles.title: After Canada loosens citizenship requirements, thousands of people apply
articles.summary: After Canada changed a law opening up citizenship opportunities, thousands of Americans are trying to become citizens of their northern neighbor. It's partly for political and personal reasons.

articles.title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
articles.summary: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Congress’s refusal to extend federal tax credits.

articles.title: Kneecap is used to controversy. On new album 'Fenian,' they're leaning further in
articles.summary: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Mo Chara and Móglaí Bap of the Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap about their new album Fenian.

articles.title: Several states — and the LA public schools — are setting limits on screen time
articles.summary: Four states have recently passed legislation to limit teaching and assessments via screens for students. So has the United States' second-largest school district.

articles.title: The expert on 'super aging' breaks down the science — and grift — in anti-aging
articles.summary: From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.

articles.title: Record-breaking DHS shutdown ends. And, May Day protests to draw crowds nationwide
articles.summary: Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.

articles.title: How well can EVs handle the heat — and the cold? AAA put them to the test
articles.summary: Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.

articles.title: After Prison, a Financial Titan Plots an Unlikely Comeback
articles.summary: Bill McGlashan served time for trying to buy his son’s way into college during the Varsity Blues scandal. He hopes his new venture will restore his name — and save the planet.

articles.title: 'Decimate' means much more today than it did in ancient Rome
articles.summary: Today, most people know the word as a synonym for "destroy." But fewer realize its origins — or that it's come to mean something strikingly different than it once did.

articles.title: Myanmar attempts to rehabilitate image with Suu Kyi move
articles.summary: State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.

articles.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
articles.summary: A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

articles.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
articles.summary: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
